name: Prompt Injection Detection
summary: Screens a user input for prompt-injection and jailbreak indicators, returning a 1 (safe) / 0 (attack detected) verdict with reasoning.
description: An LLM-as-a-Judge security classifier that analyzes a single user input for adversarial prompt attacks — prompt injection and jailbreaking. It checks for alignment manipulation (persona hijacking, instruction overriding, goal hijacking), content obfuscation and evasion (euphemism, Base64 / ROT13 / leetspeak encoding, semantic fragmentation), and structural / processing exploitation (injected instructions, delimiter abuse, resource overloading). It returns a dict with an integer 'safe' flag (1 = safe, 0 = attack detected) and a short reason.
